text: <par>Possible loan from Indic, cf. Sanskrit mārjārá.</par>
<par>ha:gom also appears in words for 'ridge (of hills)' and 'land (uneven)' so that it may have a sense of 'field' or 'mountain' when it appears in the compound for 'leopard'.</par>

<par>'kru' appears in a number of words for female animals: belleig-kru "sow";  kuak-kru "bitch"; ma:bi kru "she-goat"; ma:cu-kru "doe"; ma:◦bi-kru "goat (she)"; and tyu-kru "hen". Note, however, that a:◦shya:-kru "mithun (cow)" and klembab kru "duck" contain this morpheme and yet are not glossed as being female.</par>
